02396.summary: The plugin is being modernized around shared MIME-aware base64 utilities.
02396.content:
Current architecture direction:
- Shared utility modules under src/utils/base64 own binary/base64 encoding, MIME normalization, and image persistence decisions.
- Main plugin flows, context-menu actions, and batch conversion commands should consume those helpers instead of duplicating PNG-specific logic.
- The compatibility barrel at src/utils/conversion.ts keeps the existing import surface stable while the rest of the codebase is incrementally modernized.

03623.summary: Repo builds as a TypeScript Obsidian plugin with esbuild and focused utility tests.
03623.content:
Working commands:
- npm run build -> TypeScript compile check plus esbuild production bundle.
- npm run test:utils -> focused node:test coverage for base64, MIME, and persistence helpers.
- npm run test -> legacy obs-dev integration path.

Relevant repo files:
- manifest.json declares plugin id image-inline.
- src/main.ts is the plugin entry point.
- src/utils/base64 contains the current modernization slice.

0521a.summary: Inline data URL handling is viewport-scoped and event-driven.
0521a.content:
Editor performance pattern:
- antiLinkExpand now scans only EditorView.visibleRanges and builds decorations from viewport slices instead of serializing the full document on every update.
- cursorEscape now reacts to editor-change and selectionchange events instead of polling on an interval.
- Pure helper functions under src/coms support focused tests for data URL range detection and cursor escape behavior.

06ed6.summary: Batch conversion rewrites are editor-aware and atomically applied.
06ed6.content:
Batch conversion write pattern:
- selectAndConvert now updates open notes through the active editor so editor state stays in sync.
- Background note rewrites use vault.process with synchronous replacement application instead of direct vault.modify writes.
- Async conversion work is precomputed as replacement lists before the atomic file transform runs.

0776b.summary: Batch command debug logging uses scoped console prefixes and structured payloads.
0776b.content:
Batch command debug logging pattern:
- Batch command modals log with command-specific console prefixes so Obsidian devtools output is easy to filter.
- Success paths emit summary payloads with scope and file counts.
- Skip and failure paths log structured details for unresolved links, duplicate exports, attachment writes, and editor versus vault update paths.
- User-facing Notices remain concise while deeper operational detail stays in the console.
